calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-17-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-13-2018 Continued To Feb-06-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings, Or In The Alternative, Request For Statement Of Decision
Off Calendar May-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Le Clerc and Christopher LeClerc 415-445-0900 of Le Clerc & Le Clerc LLP for Plaintiff Steven McIlroy.
Garrett Parks, Esq. 415-248-2117 of Polsinelli for Defendant Kixeye Inc. (564372)
Mark Goodman, Esq. 415-576-3080 of Baker McKenzie for Defendant Motionloft

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court simultaneously shares his views on both Line 9 (564636 - Callen vs. Motion Loft) and Line 14 (McIlroy vs Kixeye) The matters are based on identical agreements and the tentative rulings are the same except for the names. The Court will hear argument on both cases. Plaintiff argues "Saheli' case. Defendant Motionloft argues. The Court also rules on the Objections.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument confirms the tentative ruling as stated on the record on the fees & costs provision and Injunctive relief as follows:

DEFENDANT MOTIONLOFT INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings, Or In The Alternative, Request For Statement Of Decision.

Defendant Motionloft, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is granted in its entirety. All claims alleged by plaintiff Garrett Callen must be resolved by arbitration per the parties' agreement and this case is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. Assuming without deciding that the arbitration agreement is procedurally unconscionable, it is nonetheless enforceable because it is not substantively unconscionable. The attorneys' fees and costs provision is not substantively unconscionable because it bars the arbitrator from making any fees and costs award that is prohibited by law. The venue provision is not substantively unconscionable because Santa Clara County is not very far from San Francisco and Motionloft stipulates to venue in San Francisco. Notwithstanding the absence of any specific reference to the Federal Arbitration Act, the FAA applies to this case, at least to the extent that the FAA does not differ from the California Arbitration Act, because the parties' agreement "involves interstate commerce" as that phrase is used in FAA case law. Per Saheli v. White Memorial Medical Center (2018) 21 Cal. App. 5th 308, the reference to "California law" in the arbitration agreement and in paragraph 13 of the parties' agreement refers to California law not preempted by the FAA. Because the FAA preempts Labor Code 229, that section does not apply to the parties' agreement and all of Mr. Callen's wage claims are arbitrable, notwithstanding section 229.

In addition, the tentative ruling is modified as to the fees and costs provision and Injunctive relief, as stated on the record. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for Motionloft and Kixeye are to work together and prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling on both cases in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)
